explanation: Present perfect: <has/have> + past participle. Remove the specific time reference.

correct: The cake was eaten by the dog.
explanation: Passive: object + <was/were> + past participle + <by> + agent.

correct: She said that she was tired.
explanation: Reported speech: shift tense back (<am> → <was>) and change pronouns.
Adjectives with 3+ syllables use <more> + adjective for comparatives.
Past continuous: <was/were> + verb-ing. Shows an action in progress in the past.
Short adjectives form superlatives with <the> + adjective + <-est>.

correct: I am going to study English.
explanation: Future with <going to>: subject + <am/is/are going to> + base verb.

correct: He has not finished his homework.
explanation: Present perfect negative: <has not> (hasn't) + past participle.
Past continuous questions: invert <were> and the subject.

source: When I was young, I played every day.
correct: I used to play every day.
When I was young, I used to play every day.
hint: Use <used to> + base verb for past habits
explanation: <Used to> + base verb describes past habits that no longer happen.
Past continuous: <was/were> + verb-ing for an action in progress in the past.

source: I visit my grandmother every Sunday.
explanation: Future with <will>: subject + <will> + base verb.
Short adjectives form comparatives with adjective + <-er>: <fast> → <faster>.
Short adjectives form superlatives with <the> + adjective + <-est>: <old> → <the oldest>.

correct: He said that he liked chocolate.
hint: Shift present simple to past simple
explanation: Reported speech: <like> → <liked>, <I> → <he>.
Past continuous: <were> + verb-ing for plural subjects.

correct: She is going to buy a new phone.
She's going to buy a new phone.
explanation: Future with <going to>: subject + <is/are going to> + base verb.
Long adjectives use <more> + adjective: <difficult> → <more difficult>.

correct: She said that she could swim.
explanation: Reported speech: <can> → <could>, <I> → <she>.
Present perfect: <have> + past participle. Remove specific time markers.

correct: She will work at the hospital.
explanation: Future with <will>: subject + <will> + base verb.
Irregular superlative: <good> → <the best>.
Past continuous: <was> + verb-ing.

hint: Double the final consonant before adding <-er>
explanation: Short adjectives ending in consonant-vowel-consonant double the last letter: <big> → <bigger>.

prompt: Change to negative in past simple
correct: She didn't go to the party.
She did not go to the party.
explanation: Past simple negative: <didn't> + base verb. <went> → <didn't go>.
